description: Create a content slide on future AI use in risk management with a left narrative and CRO quote sidebar plus a two-column horizontal bar chart on the right.

header.headline: Figure 13: Most significant plans for future AI use in risk management

sidebar.body:
Future plans call for increased use in all of those areas, with notably large jumps in AI-powered credit and market risk modeling and model risk management. These results largely track last year’s research, where fraud, compliance, financial crimes, credit, operational and model risk were the main priorities for using AI.

What CROs say:
“We think there is huge potential in leveraging AI, but upscaling in house is tricky. There is a lot of excitement, and it’s easy to buy or access a lot of tools. The real cost is in change management, underlying infrastructure, people and the time to do it right.”

series.label: Digital fraud and financial crime detection (e.g., AML, transaction monitoring, pattern recognition)
Cyber and operational risk management (e.g., anomaly detection, network surveillance, control testing automation)
Credit and market risk modeling (e.g., credit assessments, volatility forecasting, portfolio risk)
General use of chatbots (e.g., LLM integration with certain applications)
Legal and document analysis (e.g., contract review, documentation automation)
Model risk management (e.g., model performance, documentation, code migration)
Regulatory and compliance monitoring (e.g., automated control testing, policy alignment)
Real-time risk monitoring and early warning systems
Scenario analysis and stress testing (e.g., macroeconomic forecasting, model simulations)
Strategic decision support (e.g., aggregating risk insights, scenario planning for leadership)
Not planning to use AI in risk management within the next three years
